topic.what: Post-patch residual risk quantification in benign code using multi-signal similarity analysis
topic.how: Combines embedding-based semantic similarity, localized AST structural similarity, and cross-model agreement into unified Residual Risk Score (RRS)
topic.why_matters: Patched code may retain vulnerability-relevant properties; existing benchmarks treat patches as binary transitions ignoring residual risk

characteristics.what_it_measures: Embedding cosine similarity between vulnerable and benign code
characteristics.context: Near-unity similarity indicates limited semantic transformation post-patch

characteristics.what_it_measures: Localized TED similarity capturing fine-grained AST changes
characteristics.context: Localized TED better captures patch-relevant changes than global NTED (0.27 mean)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of high-RRS functions flagged by static analyzers
characteristics.evidence: Flagged by at least one of Cppcheck/Clang-Tidy/Facebook Infer
characteristics.context: 13 distinct residual issue categories identified including null ptr, memory errors

characteristics.evidence: Flagged by all three static analysis tools
characteristics.context: 43% flagged by two tools; 39% no warnings

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of function pairs in Quadrant I (minimal change)
characteristics.context: Identified as primary candidate space for residual risk

characteristics.what_it_measures: Maximum AST nodes for structural analysis
characteristics.context: Limits analysis to ~1088 pairs; excludes larger functions

novelty: First formalization of residual risk via representation-level proximity between vulnerable/benign code
Unified RRS combining embedding semantic, localized AST structural, and cross-model agreement signals
Demonstration that localized TED captures patch-relevant changes better than global NTED
Empirical validation showing 61% of high-RRS functions contain detectable residual issues via static analysis
open_problems: Extension to larger AST functions (>350 nodes)
Dynamic analysis integration for refined risk estimation
Generalization to other vulnerability datasets beyond PrimeVul
Automated remediation guidance for identified residual issues
